title: Validate connectedAccountId on connector OAuth callback
description: The /connectors/callback endpoint trusts the unsigned user_id query param. An attacker who knows a victim user_id could spoof a successful callback and bind a victim's connection row to an attacker-controlled Composio connected_account_id. Fix: require the (user_id, toolkit_slug, connected_account_id) tuple to match a row currently in INITIATED state — which only the authenticated /connect endpoint can create. Fixup of d3baa90. Surfaced by gemini security-high review on PR #219.

title: Fix CHECK constraint missing INITIALIZING state
description: The user_connectors CHECK constraint omits 'INITIALIZING' which is in ConnectionStatus and may be returned by Composio during init, causing a CheckViolation. Fixup of f1679d0. Surfaced by gemini review on PR #219.

title: Composio auth configs + MCP server seed script
description: Set up Composio project resources (auth configs and MCP server configs) for Notion, Linear, and GitHub. One-time setup that produces the ac_xxx and mcp_server_id values the backend will hardcode in its toolkit registry. Use Composio managed OAuth (no provider-side OAuth app registration). Allowlist 2-3 read-only tools per toolkit to start. Produces scratch/composio_seed.py (gitignored). Dry run already validated the SDK shape (see custom-connectors-dryrun-findings memo). Blocks all downstream work.
design: Per dry-run findings: one MCP server per toolkit (cleaner allowlist, easier debugging). Tool names are SCREAMING_SNAKE (e.g. NOTION_FETCH_DATA, NOTION_SEARCH_NOTION_PAGE).
acceptance_criteria: auth_config_id + mcp_server_id for each of notion/linear/github committed to backend config; seed script idempotent; documented in README.
close_reason: Seed script + registry module shipped. Dev-project IDs committed in backend/src/torale/connectors/registry.py. COMPOSIO_API_KEY wired into Settings. composio SDK added to backend deps. Seed script is idempotent; run doc in scratch/README.md.

title: Close httpx.AsyncClient instances created for MCP toolsets
description: _build_mcp_toolsets in torale-agent/server.py creates a new httpx.AsyncClient per MCP server but never closes it. Each agent run leaks file descriptors. Fix: own the clients via AsyncExitStack inside ToraleAgentExecutor.execute so they close after the run. Fixup of bd08bf2. Surfaced by gemini medium-priority review on PR #219.

title: MobileNav never renders due to startsWith('/') matching every path
acceptance_criteria: On viewport <= 768px, /dashboard /settings/* /tasks/* and other authenticated routes render MobileNav at the bottom. Landing page (/) and auth subtrees (/sign-in, /sign-up, /waitlist, /changelog) still hide it. Verify in agent-browser at 375x812.

title: Self-heal connector status drift via reconcile-on-list
description: Local user_connectors.status drifts from Composio's authoritative status whenever a callback miss leaves a row stuck INITIATED. We saw this concretely during staging validation: ca_1FMgoB3O4XVM was ACTIVE on Composio (OAuth completed) but INITIATED in our DB because the callback handler had a camelCase param bug. The handler bug is fixed (commit 9dbfb64) but any future drift (partial outage, dropped callback, network blip) leaves the same stuck state with no in-product recovery path. Today's only recovery is fresh OAuth or manual SQL via kubectl exec.
design: Edit backend/src/torale/api/routers/connectors.py list_connections (GET /connectors). It already fetches Composio status alongside local rows, but only uses Composio's status for the response payload. Add a write-back step: when Composio says ACTIVE and local says INITIATED/EXPIRED for the same ca_id (matched on user_id + toolkit_slug + connected_account_id to avoid races), UPDATE the local row. Set connected_at = NOW() when filling from null. Best-effort: if Composio list fails, skip reconciliation rather than failing the GET. Idempotent. Do NOT flip ACTIVE to EXPIRED based on Composio remote — keep that direction conservative; webhook bead torale-785 is the right surface for revocation propagation. Tests: drift case flips, same-status case is no-op, mismatched ca_id is skipped, Composio outage doesn't break GET.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/v1/connectors with a deliberately-drifted row (INITIATED locally, ACTIVE remotely for the same ca_id) returns ACTIVE AND persists the flip. Re-GET is a no-op. Composio outage during list still returns local snapshot without error.

title: Admin connector-reset endpoint
description:
Admin-only HTTP endpoint to hard-reset a user's connector state across both Composio and Torale's DB in one call. Eliminates manual kubectl-exec-python cleanup when things drift.

Motivating cases we hit during staging validation:
- Callback URL baked into a connection record pre-config-fix → reconnect reuses stuck ca_ID → localhost redirect keeps happening until the record is deleted from Composio
- Aborted OAuth flows leave INITIATED ca_IDs that Composio doesn't surface cleanly under user_id filters
- Dev-project carryover when reusing keys across envs (staging uses dev Composio today) — stale test data accumulates

Current workaround is: shell into an API pod, run a Python script against composio SDK + asyncpg. Not scalable; human time every time.
design:
POST /api/v1/admin/connectors/reset with query param user_id (UUID). Admin-gated via existing require_admin dependency (see backend/src/torale/access/auth.py).

Behavior:
1. List all Composio connections for the given user_id via composio.connected_accounts.list(user_ids=[user_id]) — iterate over full pagination, not just first page.
2. Delete each ca_ID via composio.connected_accounts.delete(ca_id). Log each deletion. Continue on individual failures; collect errors.
3. Delete all local rows: DELETE FROM user_connectors WHERE user_id = $1.
4. Return JSON: {deleted_composio: int, failed_composio: [ca_ids], deleted_local: int}.

Idempotent: safe to call against a user with zero connections (both operations become no-ops).

Do NOT bake into /connectors router — keep admin surface separate (admin router already exists at backend/src/torale/api/routers/admin.py). Follows existing pattern.

Optional add: same logic callable from a CLI in scratch/composio_cleanup.py for when a user can't auth the endpoint themselves (e.g. locked out). Low priority; can add later.

Out of scope: auto-prune scheduled job (different bead if we want it). UI button (nice-to-have; admins can hit the endpoint via browser or curl).
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/v1/admin/connectors/reset?user_id=<uuid> returns 200 with accurate counts. Non-admin caller gets 403. Idempotent: second call on empty user returns zeros without error. Verified against a real messy user on staging (Prass's current state would be a good test target before/after this bead).

title: Trust/governance posture for Composio credentials before GA
description:
Solo-maintainer trust posture for the connector feature. Composio's project-scoped API key means the Torale operator technically can act on any connected user's account. Managed OAuth brokers all share this property — we can't fix it cryptographically without rearchitecting (client-side agents or TEEs) or moving off server-hosted OAuth brokers entirely. Practical answer is trust + observability.

Context: made the staging-unblock call to use the dev Composio API key on staging (maintainer holds it). This bead captures the follow-up governance work before GA so we don't ship prod without addressing it.

Not blocking the feature ship; blocks GA / marketing.
design:
Accept it's a trust model, not a technical isolation. Make the trust explicit via governance + observability rather than pretending a different provider or separate Composio project fixes it.

Three tracks:

1. Trust doc (1 page) explaining operator access:
   - What Composio stores (OAuth tokens, connection metadata, tool-call arguments)
   - What the operator CAN do technically (list connections, generate MCP URLs, execute read-only tools on user's behalf)
   - What we commit NOT to do (use access outside task execution, share the key, etc.)
   - How we'll know (tool-call logs, monitoring)
   - Future path (enterprise Composio tier with scoped keys, multi-admin org if Torale incorporates)

2. Tool-call logging with provenance:
   - Every MCP tool call tagged user-triggered (manual re-run) vs agent-triggered (scheduler)
   - Surfaced to users on task detail + /settings/connectors
   - Lets users audit that their connections are only used for their monitors

3. Open item: email Composio support asking about per-user scoped keys or RBAC on enterprise tier. Worth 10 minutes.

Also: if/when Torale incorporates, move the Composio org to a company-owned account with a second admin.
acceptance_criteria: Trust doc published (docs-site or markdown in repo, linked from /settings/connectors); tool-call logging distinguishes user-triggered vs agent-triggered (new column or metadata field); user-visible audit log on task detail; support email sent to Composio re: scoped keys.

title: Feature flag gating for all connector UI
description: Wrap all connector surfaces (settings page, task picker section, activity badges, degradation banner) behind a feature flag. GrowthBook or whatever Torale uses currently. Per design memo §12 rollout plan: ship dark, dogfood, remove flag.
acceptance_criteria: Flag OFF hides all connector UI and connector-dependent task fields; flag ON shows full experience.
close_reason: Not a bead — inline env-var gate per UI surface, covered inside torale-0p7 and torale-awr.

title: Expired connector degradation banner on task detail
description: When a task references an EXPIRED connector, show a degradation banner on its detail page per design memo Flow D. Inline reconnect button (reuses ReconnectButton from settings page). Auto-dismisses when user reconnects and state flips to ACTIVE.
acceptance_criteria: Banner appears only for EXPIRED attached connectors; reconnect flow works from banner.

title: Activity trail MCP tool rendering
description: Extend frontend/src/components/AgentActivity.tsx to render MCP tool calls with toolkit branding per design memo §5 and §10.1 D1/D5. Uses ActivityStep.connector_slug (when present) to branch. Frontend owns slug→display-name/icon map and tool→verb map with snake-case fallback (e.g. NOTION_CREATE_COMMENT → 'create comment'). Initial map: NOTION_SEARCH_NOTION_PAGE → 'searched pages', NOTION_FETCH_DATA → 'fetched page'. No new component; extend TOOL_CONFIG.
acceptance_criteria: Activity rows for MCP tools show toolkit prefix + verb; unknown tools render with fallback transform.
close_reason: Folded into torale-krx (activity UI absorbed with backend extraction).

title: Task creation connector picker
description: Add Connected Tools section to TaskCreationDialog (dialog, not page) per design memo §4. CollapsibleSection, collapsed by default. BrutalistTable rows with checkbox multi-select. Handles EXPIRED (show warning, don't auto-unselect), no-connectors empty state (link to /settings/connectors), soft advisory for >3 selected. Selection is string[] of toolkit slugs. Task model gains attached_connector_slugs column (see torale-1p5). Form is TaskCreationDialog.tsx; task editing happens in TaskEditDialog (wire there too).
acceptance_criteria: Task saved with attached_connector_slugs field; expired connector visually flagged; empty state links to settings.
notes: Picker implemented on feat/connectors-picker (commit b5fd9f2). Frontend only: added attached_connector_slugs to Task + TaskCreatePayload, api.getConnections(), ConnectorPickerSection component, wired into TaskCreationDialog + TaskEditDialog. Collapsed by default, auto-expands on edit when task has connectors. Empty state links to /settings/connectors, expired rows flagged (not auto-unselected), >3 advisory. tsc + eslint clean. Awaiting a2i's Pydantic/router glue to land attached_connector_slugs on backend POST/PUT.
close_reason: Picker implemented + rebased onto feat/custom-connectors @ 12ab8f5. Awaiting orchestrator merge after torale-30t lands.

title: Connectors settings page (ConnectorCard grid + degradation banner)
description:
/settings/connectors as a sibling route to /settings (which remains NotificationSettingsPage). No settings shell / top tabs needed — existing notifications page stays at /settings unchanged. Connectors gets its own route.

Covers design memo §3 + Flow D (degradation banner on task detail when attached connector is EXPIRED).

Scope:
- /settings/connectors page with ConnectorCard grid
- All 5 Composio states: INACTIVE/INITIATED/ACTIVE/EXPIRED/FAILED
- Built on BrutalistCard + StatusBadge + ActionMenu
- One-click reconnect on EXPIRED
- Disconnect flow uses AlertDialog (shadcn, frontend/src/components/ui/alert-dialog.tsx) with §10.1 D2 copy
- Mid-auth: 10s poll + visibilitychange refetch per D4
- Degradation banner component on task detail page for EXPIRED attached connectors (Flow D)
- Inline env-var/role gate (no GrowthBook in codebase) — VITE_ENABLE_CONNECTORS=1 or user.publicMetadata.role === 'admin'
- Microcopy per memo §3. Never says Composio/MCP/gateway/token in UI.
acceptance_criteria: End-to-end Notion connect flow works against live backend; all 5 state treatments implemented; reconnect + disconnect both functional.

title: Settings shell with top-tab navigation
description: Add /settings shell with top-tab navigation between /settings/notifications and /settings/connectors. /settings redirects to /settings/notifications. Uses FilterGroup component for tabs per design memo §2. Feature-local SettingsTabs component (candidate for promotion to shared later).
acceptance_criteria: Existing notifications page unchanged; new connectors tab renders placeholder; mobile responsive.
close_reason: Folded into torale-0p7. Exploration showed there's no /settings shell today — NotificationSettingsPage is /settings. Connectors gets sibling route /settings/connectors, no top-tab nav to build.

title: Activity trail MCP tool rendering (backend extraction + frontend TOOL_CONFIG)
description:
Ship the MCP activity story end-to-end.

Backend (torale-agent/tools.py):
- Extend extract_activity() to populate connector_slug (from TOOLKIT_* tool-name prefix, lowercased) and annotations on ActivityStep
- Add prefix-based entries to _TOOL_INPUT_KEYS for MCP tools (e.g. NOTION_* → query)

Frontend (frontend/src/components/AgentActivity.tsx):
- Extend TOOL_CONFIG with entries for allowlisted MCP tools (NOTION_SEARCH_NOTION_PAGE, NOTION_FETCH_DATA)
- Add slug→icon and tool→verb maps per §10.1 D5 with snake-case fallback
- Conditional render for step.connector_slug per §10.1 D1

Backend + frontend changes ship together; no contract staging needed since ActivityStep fields are additive + optional.
acceptance_criteria: Agent run using Notion connector produces ActivityStep with connector_slug='notion' and tool='NOTION_SEARCH_NOTION_PAGE'.

title: Per-run MCP agent assembly in torale-agent
description: In torale-agent/server.py, build Pydantic AI Agent per-run when metadata.mcp_servers is non-empty: inject MCP clients with x-api-key header. Cache a no-connectors agent for the empty-list common path (hot path, per dry-run findings on URL shape and SSE transport). Follow 307 redirects (SDK does this automatically).
acceptance_criteria: Agent successfully calls Notion tools via MCP in dev; fallback path unchanged; warm-path latency not regressed.
close_reason:
Agent assembly shipped in torale-agent/server.py.

- _build_mcp_toolsets(): turns A2A metadata.mcp_servers into a list of MCPServerStreamableHTTP toolsets with a per-toolset httpx.AsyncClient(follow_redirects=True, headers={x-api-key: COMPOSIO_API_KEY}). Redirect flag is load-bearing — Composio /v3/mcp/{id} 307s to /v3/mcp/{id}/mcp and the default MCP client does not follow.
- ToraleAgentExecutor.execute(): calls agent.run(... toolsets=...) with the per-run toolsets. Per-call toolsets means we keep the single cached agent for the no-MCP hot path. Empty metadata → toolsets=None → equivalent to pre-a2i behavior.
- pyproject: pydantic-ai-slim[google,mcp] extras (adds mcp package).

Live-tested: Gemini successfully called NOTION_SEARCH_NOTION_PAGE twice against live Composio MCP, retry with different query, fallback to memory, completed cleanly. No-MCP path unchanged.

title: A2A metadata extension — pass mcp_servers to agent
description: Extend scheduler/agent.py metadata to include mcp_servers list derived from user's ACTIVE connectors attached to the task. Resolved at run time (not frozen at task-save). Shape: mcp_servers=[{toolkit, url, api_key_env}]. Generate per-user MCP URL via composio client wrapper. Empty list for tasks without connectors (vast majority).
acceptance_criteria: Metadata correctly populated for task with connectors; empty for tasks without.
close_reason:
A2A metadata extension shipped.

Changes:
- alembic c5e8f9d23b41: tasks.attached_connector_slugs TEXT[] NOT NULL DEFAULT ARRAY[]::TEXT[]
- TaskBase/TaskUpdate/TaskData: attached_connector_slugs field (round-trips POST/PUT/GET)
- INSERT in tasks router updated (UPDATE generic loop already supports the field)
- scheduler/connector_resolution.py: resolve_mcp_servers (intersect task.attached_slugs × user_connectors WHERE status=ACTIVE, generates per-user MCP URLs) + mark_connectors_used (updates last_used_at post-run)
- scheduler/agent.py: call_agent + _call_agent_internal accept mcp_servers list, forward in A2A metadata (nested under 'mcp_servers' key, omitted when empty)
- scheduler/job.py: fetch attached_connector_slugs + user_id, resolve connectors, pass to call_agent, mark as used after run

Empty-list common path: short-circuits before DB call. 227 tests pass. Agent side backwards-compat (unknown metadata keys ignored until torale-30t lands).

title: Composio expiry webhook handler
description: Subscribe to Composio connection-expiry webhook and handle incoming events. On EXPIRED event, update user_connectors.status and status_reason. Verify webhook signature (exact scheme TBD — dry run did not test this). Expose subscription setup via the seed script or one-time setup.
acceptance_criteria: Manual revoke via Notion settings triggers EXPIRED state in DB within expected window.

title: Connector REST endpoints + backend callback route
description: API endpoints for the connector management flow. POST /connectors/{toolkit}/connect returns the Composio redirect URL. GET /connectors returns current user's connections with status. DELETE /connectors/{toolkit} revokes connection. GET /connectors/available returns seeded toolkits list (slug, display_name, description, allowed_tools). GET /connectors/callback is a backend-rendered HTML page that verifies the Composio callback (status, connected_account_id, user_id passthrough), updates user_connectors, and returns a you-can-close-this-tab page. Per design memo §3 and §10.1 D2.
acceptance_criteria: All endpoints work against the dev Composio project; callback idempotent; HTML page matches design copy.
close_reason: Router at backend/src/torale/api/routers/connectors.py with 5 endpoints: GET /connectors/available, GET /connectors, POST /{toolkit_slug}/connect, DELETE /{toolkit_slug}, GET /callback (backend-rendered HTML). Uses torale-ehz client wrapper. Writes user_connectors rows via asyncpg. Callback is idempotent (UPDATE-only). settings.backend_url added for callback URL construction. All 227 tests still pass.

title: Composio client wrapper module
description: Thin async wrapper around composio SDK in backend (torale/integrations/composio.py). Functions: initiate_connection(user_id, toolkit), get_connection_status(connected_account_id), generate_mcp_url(user_id, toolkit), list_user_connections(user_id), delete_connection(connected_account_id), verify_webhook(payload, signature). Hides SDK object shapes behind simple dict/dataclass returns. Uses settings.composio_api_key.
acceptance_criteria: Module tested with live Composio in dev project; SDK version pinned in pyproject.toml.
close_reason: Async wrapper module at backend/src/torale/connectors/client.py. Functions: initiate_connection, get_connection, list_user_connections, delete_connection, generate_mcp_url, verify_webhook (stub — real impl deferred with torale-785). All wrap sync SDK in asyncio.to_thread. Return Pydantic models (Connection, ConnectionInitiation, MCPInstance) so consumers don't couple to SDK shapes. Live-tested against dev Composio project: list/get/generate/initiate/delete all round-trip cleanly.

title: user_connectors table + ActivityStep schema additions
description: New user_connectors table and additive fields on ActivityStep. Alembic migration. Per dry-run findings and design memo §10.1 D1. user_connectors columns: id, user_id (FK), toolkit_slug, connected_account_id (ca_xxx), status (ACTIVE/INITIATED/EXPIRED/FAILED/INACTIVE), last_used_at, connected_at, status_reason. ActivityStep gains optional connector_slug and annotations fields (ships unrendered in v1 per D3). Must mirror changes in torale-agent/models.py per CLAUDE.md schema-sync rule (D6).
acceptance_criteria: Alembic migration applies cleanly; both ActivityStep copies in sync; Pydantic models updated.
close_reason: Landed on feat/custom-connectors-migration as ba324df, merged into feat/custom-connectors via fb3052a. Migration, user_connectors table, ActivityStep.connector_slug + annotations, ToolAnnotations model all in. 227 tests pass on merged branch.

title: Dogfood + changelog + flag removal
description: Final rollout steps. End-to-end dogfood with real Notion workspace. Write changelog entry for backend/static/changelog.json. Remove feature flag. Announce.
acceptance_criteria: Changelog deployed; flag removed; at least one real task running with a real connector end-to-end.
close_reason: Not real work — changelog + gate removal happens naturally post-dogfood.
